Responsibilities.Item: Maintain source manifests and import logs for reference data.
Reject LLM-generated content as evidence.
Reject user decisions as evidence for normative FSNB fields or official KSI codes.
Create structured questions when required evidence is missing.

Invariants.Item: No source — no rule.
source_origin=llm_generated implies source_authority=forbidden.
Files in inbox are not trusted until accepted into raw with checksum.

Responsibilities.Item: Represent each imported source version as reference_release.
Represent stable logical records as reference_object.
Create reference_object_revision only when canonical payload hash changes, or records are added/deleted.
Create reference_change_set and reference_change_detail entries for added, changed, deleted, and unchanged records.

Invariants.Item: Unchanged records reuse previous revisions.
Allowed operations are add, update, delete.
Allowed change types are added, changed, deleted, unchanged.

Responsibilities.Item: Store draft and candidate matching rules with explicit evidence slots.
Prevent active status when required evidence is missing.
Track dependencies on reference objects and revisions.
Move rules to requires_norm_review when depended normative reference revisions change.

Invariants.Item: ModelElement must not map directly to GESNNorm.
KSIResultCode must not directly select GESNNorm.
GESNNorm must not create ScheduleTask directly.
Missing evidence blocks active status.

Responsibilities.Item: Maintain project plan, artifact registry, traceability matrix, decision log, and status report.
Prepare acceptance reports for execution packets.
Generate acceptance and user action dashboards from source files.
Generate verification dashboards from verification plans and monthly test protocols.
Validate artifact paths, expected artifacts, acceptance reports, and acceptance ownership rules.
Validate accepted artifact protection and user approval requirements.
Prevent Codex from marking packets accepted.

Invariants.Item: Codex may set ready_for_acceptance but must not set accepted.
accepted_by must not be Codex.
accepted status requires acceptance_decision=accepted and non-empty accepted_by.
decided_by must not be Codex.
Accepted artifacts are protected and require explicit user approval before material modification.
Manual verification checks must not be marked checked or passed by Codex.
checked_by must not be Codex.
Every created artifact must be registered in docs/artifact-registry.yml.

Responsibilities.Item: Classify Tartip execution packet impact on dissertation sections.
Maintain dissertation impact log and section update queue.
Generate controlled prompts for user review.
Validate prompt metadata, forbidden claims, and DOCX update guardrails.
Prepare acceptance report for dissertation synchronization artifacts.

Forbidden.Item: direct DOCX editing without explicit user request
bibliography renumbering without explicit user request
modifying accepted artifacts without user approval

Invariants.Item: DOCX update requires accepted markdown patch and explicit user request.
New bibliography sources require citation requests before bibliography changes.
Forbidden claims must not appear in generated prompts as assertions.
accepted_by must not be Codex.

Responsibilities.Item: Maintain audit profile, audit reports, language policy, and audit findings registry.
Check Codex guardrails for domain logic, reference evidence, acceptance ownership, dashboard integrity, verification ownership, monthly planning, and dissertation sync.
Preserve existing user statuses and resolutions in audit-findings.yml.
Surface open audit findings in the user action dashboard.

Invariants.Item: Audit-first packets must not mass-rewrite existing documents.
Medium and low language findings must not block make check.
Critical findings must block the corresponding audit command.
Accepted/protected artifacts must not be modified without user approval.
Audit scripts must not reset acknowledged, fixed, accepted_risk, false_positive, blocked, or requires_user_approval finding statuses to open.
User-facing audit reports must be written in Russian where possible.

Responsibilities.Item: Document branch naming, branch switching, merge readiness, merge prohibitions, and forbidden Git files.
Validate current Git state in advisory and strict modes without mutating Git state.
Warn about mixed execution packet scopes in the working tree.
Prevent merge readiness when acceptance is pending or user approval is missing.

Forbidden.Item: adding secrets, dumps, real DOCX/PDF dissertation files, or real normative bases to Git

Invariants.Item: Branch names should follow ep-<number>-<short-slug> unless a documented exception exists.
Merge requires acceptance_decision=accepted and accepted_by not empty.
accepted_by must not be Codex.
Advisory mode must not fail only because of warnings in the current dirty baseline.
Strict mode may fail on warnings before merge preparation.

Responsibilities.Item: Aggregate active user review items into one Markdown and YAML workbench.
Show ready_for_acceptance packets, pending manual checks, open user actions, requires_user_approval items, critical/high audit findings, and active blockers.
Hide accepted packets from active_review_items while preserving accepted history.
Validate source_file, source_checksum_sha256, target decision files, and user-owned fields.
Apply explicit user acceptance decisions to acceptance reports only through a safe script.

Forbidden.Item: replacing acceptance reports as source of truth
accepting packets on behalf of the user
changing README.md or CHANGELOG.md for language normalization

Invariants.Item: Accepted packets must not appear in active_review_items.
Accepted packets may appear only in recently_accepted for reference.
Acceptance reports remain source of truth for acceptance decisions.
accepted_by, checked_by, answered_by, and decided_by must not be Codex.
Stale source checksums block decision application.
Pseudo blockers and pseudo risks are forbidden.

Responsibilities.Item: Reflect accepted user decisions from acceptance reports into execution packet statuses, dashboards, and planning documents.
Keep accepted reports as the source of truth for user acceptance decisions.
Keep accepted packets out of active acceptance queues while preserving accepted history.
Expose post-acceptance debt without closing verification checks, user actions, or audit findings.
Validate accepted_baseline state through scripts/validate_post_acceptance_state.py.

Forbidden.Item: changing accepted reports EP-001 through EP-012
closing user actions or data requirements

Invariants.Item: Accepted packet status must reflect acceptance_decision=accepted and non-empty accepted_by in the acceptance report.
EP-001 through EP-012 must not appear in active acceptance queue after user acceptance.
EP-013 remains ready_for_acceptance until the user accepts it.
Pending verification checks remain post-acceptance debt and do not reopen accepted reports.
protected_accepted_artifacts=0 remains visible until a future protection packet classifies protection flags.

Responsibilities.Item: Require explicit --dry-run and --apply modes for scripts/apply_user_review_decisions.py.
Keep dry-run and default execution non-writing.
Show planned changes, affected files, reasons, and diffs before any write.
Generate workbench instructions that require dry-run review before apply.
Apply only explicitly filled user acceptance decisions from docs/user-review-workbench.yml.
Reject stale source checksums, accepted_by=Codex, empty user fields, active blockers, and already accepted report overwrites.
Use atomic writes when applying accepted user decisions.
Keep accepted artifact protection planning references on EP-018, not EP-014.

Forbidden.Item: accepting packets on behalf of Codex
overwriting already accepted reports without explicit user approval
applying manual verification, user action, audit finding, or requires_user_approval items
changing acceptance criteria, blockers, risks, artifacts, or unrelated sections
changing acceptance decisions EP-001 through EP-013

Invariants.Item: Default execution is equivalent to dry-run and must not modify files.
--dry-run must not change git status --short.
--apply requires acceptance_decision=accepted, accepted_by, accepted_at, and comments.
Generated workbench flow must list dry-run before apply.
accepted_by must not be Codex.
Stale source_checksum_sha256 blocks apply.
Accepted reports remain source of truth after user-owned apply.

Responsibilities.Item: Classify accepted source/manual artifacts in docs/artifact-registry.yml.
Classify generated dashboards, workbench files, and audit reports as derived artifacts.
Validate that protected accepted artifacts require explicit user approval before future changes.
Maintain docs/protected-artifact-change-requests.yml as the registry for approved protected artifact changes.
Prevent EP-010-LANGUAGE-NORMALIZATION from modifying protected accepted artifacts without approved change request.

Forbidden.Item: changing acceptance reports EP-001 through EP-014
changing acceptance_decision, accepted_by, accepted_at, comments, checked_by, answered_by, or decided_by
hard-locking generated dashboards or derived reports

Invariants.Item: Accepted acceptance reports must have protection_status=protected.
Protected artifacts must have change_requires_user_approval=true.
Generated dashboards must not have protection_status=protected.
Generated artifacts must declare generator, derived_from, and regeneration_command.
Approved protected artifact changes require an approved change request before modification.
accepted_by, decided_by, checked_by, and answered_by must not be Codex.
